Off Calendar Jan-08-2018 Continued To Mar-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended Notice Of Demurrer
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sean Crandall, of Strategic Legal Practices, Tel. 310.929.4900, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Bailey J. Langner, of King & Spalding LLP, Tel. 415.318.1200, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Amended Demurrer as to the seventh cause of action for concealment in the complaint filed by plaintiff Ignacio Barrientos is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Mr. Barrientos has leave to allege ultimate facts showing tolling, delayed discovery, estoppel and/or any other basis to avoid the statute of limitations. The allegations in paragraph 6 are conclusory. Ford's other arguments lack merit. Mr. Barrientos can allege a viable claim for concealment even if he did not have direct dealings with Ford. (Jones v. ConocoPhillips (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1187, 1199-1200.) The concealment cause of action does not need to be alleged with the same degree of particularity as an intentional misrepresentation cause of action. (Id. at 1199.). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR No. 6840; Dept. 302.

Amended Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sean Crandall, of Strategic Legal Practices, Tel. 310.929.4900, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Bailey J. Langner, of King & Spalding LLP, Tel. 415.318.1200, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Amended Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint as to the prayer for punitive damages in the
complaint filed by plaintiff Ignacio Barriento is granted with twenty days leave to amend. The concealment cause of action is the only claim that supports a prayer for punitive damages and a demurrer to that claim has been sustained with leave to amend. (De Anza Santa Cruz Mobile Estates Homeowners Assn. v. De Anza Santa Cruz Mobile Estates (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 890, 915.). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR No. 6840; Dept. 302.

DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Continued to December 15, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/Law and Motion per the agreement of the parties.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org. (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-07-2017 Continued To Jan-24-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Sep-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is DENIED: The sole defendant in this case is a corporate defendant. A corporation may be sued in the county where the obligation or liability arises, or in the county where the principal place of business of such corporation is situated, subject to the power of the court to change the place of trial as in other cases. C.C.P. 395.5. Venue is presumed proper in the absence of facts to the contrary. There are no facts supporting Defendant's request for a change of venue to Contra Costa County Superior Court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Gail Dekreon; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)
